content.full_text:
21 July 92

To: Fair Political Practices Commission:
Technical Assistance & Analysis Div.
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95812-0807
Attn: Sandra

From: Jane Granzer
300-G-Avenue
Coronado, California 92118

Re: Detailed Campaign Information

While at the San Diego County Registrar's Office, seeking clarification on some issues, Linda called you. Three candidates, one running for Coronado Mayor and two for Council seats, wish to form a committee which was discussed with you. Linda suggested that I write to you for some detailed clarification on how to fill out some of the reports. As Treasurer, I do not want to jeopardize any of the candidates with my mistakes.

The Coronado City Clerk states that there is no limitation on contributions and verified this with the City Manager which she told me today: all contributions over $5.00 must be accounted.

The election will be on November 3, 1992. I especially need to know how to handle forms 501 & 502 which we (Linda) neglected to clarify. On form 501—how do candidates make and get return on loans? Is the Tax Board the one to contact for I.D. number or does this come from the Secretary of State from form number 410?

I appreciate your help and will be most grateful for any and all detailed information you can send.

Respectfully requested by
[signature]

The image appears to be a photograph of a handwritten letter or note, but the handwriting is extremely faint and mostly illegible. Only a few scattered words and numbers are partially visible, such as "800-6-YAWARE," "From: TRUE CANCER," and some numbers and letters that are mostly not fully legible. Due to the quality and faintness of the ink, most of the content cannot be reliably transcribed accurately.
